items.question: How does text become noisy audio?
items.answer: Prompts are scanned for texture words (squelch, buzz, honk), timing (short hit vs. tail), and space (dry vs. roomy). Models synthesize matching waveforms, then gentle processing reins in peaks. Iterate by nudging adjectives and regenerating. items.question: How should I prompt for clearer gags?
items.answer: Pair the noise type with context: "wet chew + spit-take," "springy door + cartoon eyes." Mention duration ("250 ms poke") and dynamics ("soft then loud"). Concrete beats vague: compare "rubber chicken squeeze" with "goofy squeeze toy." Lead prompts with sound effect funny honk when you need instant cartoon clarity.
